---
title: "Phase 6b — Full population at scale (N=100, 1-arm baseline)"
format: html
params:
  seed: 42
  ticks: 5
  corpus_path: "../../data/personas_corpus_n100.json"
  topology_k: 4
  topology_p: 0.1
  max_concurrency: 16
  cluster_threshold: 0.15
  run: false        # live-spend guard: flip to true (or via the runner) to execute
---

## Aim

The first scale run of the real population: **100 census-seeded, disposition-anchored
Americans** (the P6a corpus) talking about daylight saving time over their small-world
neighbourhood for `ticks` ticks, then surveyed. One arm — the *pure* population: **no
external feed, no pre-polarised actors** (the ADR-0010 feed and the seed-time conviction
injection are the deliberate *next* step, held out so this baseline is interpretable).

This satisfies the Phase-6 DoD (a full 100-agent run completes with clean logs; R5/R6/R17
re-validated at scale) and reads the P5 validation instrument at N=100: does a
disposition-rich population hold its voice diversity over the run, or homogenise? The
**R27 action-space gate is read first** — a convergence number is only trusted once the
SPEAK/ABSTAIN space is shown to be genuinely exercised.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
from plotnine import *  # noqa: F403

from polis import metrics
from polis.embeddings import EmbeddingModel
from polis.llm import LLMClient, LLMConfig
from polis.questions import DST_OPTIONS, DST_QUESTION
from polis.runlog import RunLog
from polis.scheduler import SchedulerConfig
from polis.simulation import DynamicsConfig, Population, Simulation
from polis.topology import SmallWorld

P = {
    "seed": 42, "ticks": 5, "corpus_path": "../../data/personas_corpus_n100.json",
    "topology_k": 4, "topology_p": 0.1, "max_concurrency": 16, "cluster_threshold": 0.15,
    "run": False,
}
SUPPORT = len(DST_OPTIONS)  # 4 — entropy normaliser (R14)
```

## Build the population from the corpus (no seed-time LLM cost)

`Population.from_corpus` loads the cached, content-hashed persona artifact and seeds each
agent's private memory at t=0 — no generation happens here. The agent *decides* run on the
baseline `qwen/qwen3-32b`; the corpus's larger seed-time models are already baked in.

```{python}
embedder = EmbeddingModel()                 # local BGE — seeds memories + embeds utterances
client = LLMClient(LLMConfig(model="qwen/qwen3-32b", reasoning=False))
population = Population.from_corpus(P["corpus_path"], client=client, embedder=embedder)
print("agents:", len(population.agents), "| corpus hash:", population.corpus_meta.get("content_hash", "")[:16])
population.agents[0].persona.system_prompt()[:200]
```

## Configure the simulation (small-world, R4/R10; R28 simultaneous)

`SmallWorld(k=4, p=0.1)`: each agent hears ~4 ring neighbours plus a few long-range
rewires — high clustering, short paths. Chosen because full connectivity collapses to
unanimity by tick 1 (P4), while small-world preserves distinct communities (R10). The run
log is file-backed so the trajectory is durable (R15/R17).

```{python}
topology = SmallWorld(k=P["topology_k"], p=P["topology_p"], seed=P["seed"])
dynamics = DynamicsConfig(update_scheme="simultaneous", seed=P["seed"])
sim = Simulation(
    population,
    topology=topology,
    dynamics=dynamics,
    logger=RunLog.open("../../data/phase6_scale.sqlite"),
    scheduler_config=SchedulerConfig(max_concurrency=P["max_concurrency"]),
)
sim._build_config(P["ticks"])["topology"]  # the versioned topology descriptor (R17)
```

## Run (live) — the one expensive cell

```{python}
if P["run"]:
    run = sim.run(P["ticks"])
    print("run_id:", run.run_id, "| config_hash:", run.config_hash[:16])
    print("throughput:", {k: run.throughput[k] for k in ("n_calls", "wall_s", "decides_per_s", "latency_p95_s", "failures")})
else:
    run = None
    print("run=False — flip params.run to execute the live N=100 run.")
```

## §1 — R27 action-space adequacy (read FIRST)

Before any convergence number: is SPEAK/ABSTAIN actually being exercised? A degenerate
action space (all-abstain, single-stance, repeated wording) caps observable divergence in
a way the baseline can't catch (R27). No flags → the divergence numbers are real.

```{python}
if run is not None:
    adq = metrics.action_space_adequacy(run, stances=list(DST_OPTIONS), embedder=embedder,
                                        threshold=P["cluster_threshold"])
    print({k: adq[k] for k in ("n_actions", "abstain_rate", "distinct_stances_used",
                               "stance_coverage", "utterance_uniqueness")})
    print("FLAGS:", adq["flags"] or "none — action space genuinely exercised")
```

## §2 — Divergence trajectory (both axes, per tick, R15)

Categorical (dominant-share / entropy over the 4 DST stances) beside continuous
(pairwise dispersion / cluster-count over utterance embeddings). The categorical axis
answers *what they conclude*; the embedding axis answers *whether their voices stay
distinct* — the two can disagree (the P5 finding).

```{python}
if run is not None:
    traj = pd.DataFrame(metrics.divergence_trajectory(run, embedder, support=SUPPORT,
                                                       threshold=P["cluster_threshold"]))
    display(traj[["tick", "n", "dominant_share", "entropy", "n_utterances",
                  "pairwise_dispersion", "cluster_count"]])
    traj.to_csv("../../data/phase6_scale_trajectory.csv", index=False)
```

```{python}
if run is not None:
    long = traj.melt(id_vars="tick",
                     value_vars=["dominant_share", "entropy", "pairwise_dispersion", "cluster_count"],
                     var_name="metric", value_name="value")
    print(
        ggplot(long, aes("tick", "value"))
        + geom_line() + geom_point()
        + facet_wrap("metric", scales="free_y")
        + labs(title="Divergence trajectory — N=100 full-corpus population",
               x="tick", y="")
        + theme_minimal()
    )
```

## §3 — Endpoint survey (R18) + summary

A separately-invoked survey of the live population (not folded into the tick loop, R18).
`return_skipped` surfaces any agents dropped after retries, so the coverage is auditable.

```{python}
if run is not None:
    summary = metrics.divergence_summary(run, embedder, support=SUPPORT, threshold=P["cluster_threshold"])
    print("endpoint divergence:", summary)

    answers, skipped = population.survey(DST_QUESTION, return_skipped=True)
    print(f"\nsurvey coverage: {len(answers)}/{len(population.agents)} (skipped: {skipped or 'none'})")
    dist = pd.Series([a["choice"] for a in answers]).value_counts()
    display(dist)
    dist.to_csv("../../data/phase6_scale_survey.csv")
```

## Verdict

*(Filled after the live run.)* Read in order: R27 gate clean? → does the categorical
axis converge (dominant-share → ?) → does the embedding axis hold voice diversity
(cluster-count / dispersion trajectory)? The disposition-rich population's voice-diversity
trajectory is the headline; the survey distribution is the population's DST estimate (to be
calibrated against a US poll at P7). Baseline for the *next* unit: turn on the ADR-0010
feed + seed-time conviction injection and measure the delta against this run.
